name: Mugar Memorial Library
description: Main research library. Quiet floors on 3–5. Has printing, group study rooms on floor 2, and a 24-hour reading room during finals.

name: Science & Engineering Library (SCI)
description: Library focused on STEM resources. Quieter than Mugar, good for focused work. Printing available.

name: GSU Study Lounge (2nd Floor)
description: Open study area above the GSU food court. Great for quick work sessions between classes. Tends to get noisy during lunch.

name: CDS 3rd Floor Study Lounge
description: Modern study lounge with collaborative and quiet sections. Whiteboards available. Very popular with CS and data science students.

name: Questrom School of Business — Atrium
description: Open atrium with seating on multiple levels. Good natural light, collaborative atmosphere. Bloomberg terminals on lower level.

name: Photonics Center Study Lounge
description: Quiet lounge in the engineering quad. Less crowded than Mugar. Good spot if you're near ENG or CDS.

name: GSU Starbucks
description: Fast coffee and snacks. Usually busy 8–10am and noon. Seating available inside.

name: Panda Express (GSU)
description: Fast Chinese-American food. Accepts BU Dining Points. Quick option between classes.

name: Rhett's (Fitness & Recreation Center)
description: Casual café inside FitRec. Good smoothies and snacks. Quiet enough to eat and study.

name: The Diner (West Campus)
description: All-you-can-eat dining hall. Best value if you have a meal plan. Often less crowded than GSU during peak hours.

name: Granby St. (CDS Area Dining)
description: Dining spot near the CDS/Questrom cluster. Grab-and-go sandwiches, hot food, and coffee. Good for quick bites.

name: Mugar Library Printer (Floor 1)
description: Black & white and color printing. Pay with BU ID. Computers available nearby for file access.

name: GSU Computing Lab (2nd Floor)
description: Computing lab with ~30 computers and printers. BU ID swipe required. Good fallback if you forgot your laptop.

name: CDS Computing Lab
description: Computing and printing in the CDS building. Usually less crowded than Mugar or GSU labs.

name: ERC (Educational Resource Center) — Tutoring
description: Free drop-in and appointment tutoring for most undergrad subjects. Especially strong in STEM. Walk-in hours vary by subject.

name: BU Career Development — Drop-In Hours
description: 15-minute drop-in career advising for resume review, cover letters, and quick job search questions. No appointment needed during drop-in.

name: Dean of Students Office
description: Student support for academic difficulty, personal issues, medical leaves, and emergency resources.

name: International Students & Scholars Office (ISSO)
description: Visa advising, OPT/CPT applications, travel signatures, and support for all international students. Book appointments online.

name: FitRec (Fitness & Recreation Center)
description: Large gym and recreation center. Has quiet seating areas near the track on the upper level. Good mental health break between classes.

name: Agganis Arena Study Lounge
description: Quiet lounge near the arena entrance. Rarely crowded. Good for a solo work session near West Campus.

name: Stone Science Building Atrium
description: Open atrium in the CAS science building. High ceilings, natural light, good for reading. Near BioMedical Engineering dept.

name: COM (College of Communication) Lounge
description: Media-focused lounge with editing suites nearby. Good for journalism or media production students.

name: Marsh Chapel Meditation Room
description: Quiet meditation and reflection space. No reservation needed. Good for decompressing between stressful days.

name: Warren Towers Convenience Store
description: Late-night snack option near central campus. Open later than most dining options.

name: Cummington Mall Study Benches
description: Outdoor seating along Cummington Mall. Weather permitting. Good for phone calls or outdoor reading.
